paragraphs.text: I am happy to express my view on the movement of multinationals not to guarantee the right to hold our games. I do not understand that I buy a game in physical format but cannot play it months or years later. Like something purchased, e.g. Carro, not just one year later, you cannot use it just because the manufacturer does not want to honour the commitment to provide maintenance and/or support to be able to use it. It is not fair for manufacturers of games or other products to be able to do so without any form of punishment. If I buy something I buy something, not from the company that made it, otherwise I was renting and buying it. The fact that those undertakings are able to write what they wanted in use politics without being controlled and punished is uncritical. It is also incredible that I can play a game of 1995 in 2025 and cannot play a game of 2024 in 2025. In my sincere opinion if you buy the game in the physical format, it is myself and no company or manufacturer should have the power to cancel, block or change the same game years later simply because the game is no longer profitable, I also consider this to be a gateway for other manufacturers to do the same. What will be the next? Block the car at full motorway only because the manufacturer does not support the software further?
